caption.text: I’ll give you a UX shortcut you never see anywhere, but it’ll make your SaaS
addictive. Turn every completed step into a power-up.
Let me explain.
Most SaaS give access to everything from day one
and for some, that’s necessary.
But for others,
you can create a onboarding path when they first enter the SaaS
to guide users
however you want.
Imagine your user has just added their first client.
The classic scenario is: they click on “add,”
they enter the name,
the info, and they confirm.
The client appears in the list and then nothing else happens.
It’s up to the user to figure out what they can do with that client.
Now imagine the same scenario, but when they add the client,
there’s an animation that says: “You just unlocked:
quote creation.”
There’s a preview of the feature that animates, plus a CTA
personalized: “Create your first quote for…”
and the client’s name.
This second scenario completely changes the game.
First, because you create a sense of progress, like in a video game.
In the end, the brain loves unlocking abilities.
Then because you naturally guide them to the next logical step.
You never leave your user hanging.
And finally, because you personalize the journey.
The CTA includes the name of the client they just added.
So to apply this little hack,
it’s super simple:
identify your ideal user journey. For example,
add a client, create a quote,
send an invoice. And turn each step into unlocking the next.
Client added,
unlock quote creation. Quote created,
unlock invoice creation. Invoice sent,
unlock payment follow-up. And each time, add an animation,
a preview, and a personalized CTA.
Result:
when your user first uses your product, they never stop,
they’re always guided to the next logical action.
And that’s how a passive user becomes an engaged user.
